post.summary: Create a new record in cook_logs

outcome.description: dropdown: choose one of [queued, starting, cooking, completed, aborted, failed]

id.description: ID of the newly created record

get.description:
### Filtering

You can filter results by adding column-based query parameters directly to the URL.
Operators use bracket notation:

| Operator | Example | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `field=value` | `?status=active` | Equal (default) |
| `field[ne]=value` | `?status[ne]=inactive` | Not equal |
| `field[gt]=value` | `?price[gt]=100` | Greater than |
| `field[gte]=value` | `?date[gte]=2024-05-01` | Greater than or equal |
| `field[lt]=value` | `?score[lt]=500` | Less than |
| `field[lte]=value` | `?score[lte]=800` | Less than or equal |
| `field[in]=value` | `?type[in]=a,b,c` | In list (comma-separated) |
| `field[like]=value` | `?name[like]=john` | Partial match |

**Available columns:** `user_id`, `device_id`, `recipe_id`, `recipe_title`, `order_id`, `outcome`, `started_at`, `finished_at`, `aborted_at`, `telemetry_json`, `summary`, `created_at`

**Example:**
```
?Instance={db_name}&status=active&price[gte]=100&price[lte]=500&sort=price&order=desc&page=1&limit=20
```

parameters.name: queryParams
parameters.description: Filters, pagination & sorting as key=value pairs separated by &. Example: status=active&page=2&limit=5&sort=name&order=asc. Available columns: user_id, device_id, recipe_id, recipe_title, order_id, outcome, started_at, finished_at, aborted_at, telemetry_json, summary, created_at

outcome.description: dropdown: choose one of [queued, starting, cooking, completed, aborted, failed]

get.summary: Retrieve a record by ID from cook_logs

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the record to retrieve

outcome.description: dropdown: choose one of [queued, starting, cooking, completed, aborted, failed]

dropdown: choose one of [queued, starting, cooking, completed, aborted, failed]

post.description:
### Bulk Create Guide
Insert multiple records into the database with a single API call.

- **Max Limit**: 500 records per request.
- **Performance**: Highly recommended over looping single POST requests.

**Response**: Returns an HTTP 207 Multi-Status with a summary and a per-record breakdown of successes and failures.

records.description: An array of objects to insert

outcome.description: dropdown: choose one of [queued, starting, cooking, completed, aborted, failed]

207.description: Multi-Status response indicating success/failure per record

put.description:
### Bulk Update Guide
Update multiple existing records simultaneously using a single transaction.

- **Requirement**: Every object in the `records` array MUST include the primary key (`id`).
- **Max Limit**: 500 records per request.
- **Atomic**: Wraps updates in a transaction (all-or-nothing per database rules).

records.description: An array of objects to update. Must include the primary key.

outcome.description: dropdown: choose one of [queued, starting, cooking, completed, aborted, failed]

207.description: Multi-Status response indicating success/failure per record

delete.description:
### Bulk Delete Guide
Delete multiple records by providing the IDs of all the records you want to delete.

- **Max Limit**: 500 IDs per request.

ids.description: An array of primary keys to delete
